title: Bulbophyllum tripudianthe

The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum tripudianthe include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
